tokens: (Pause) (Perhaps looking off to the west, toward the very large rock 4.5 kilometers west of them) Boy, is that a big rock.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I'll be there in a minute.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Houston, how long you going to let us stay out?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: How long you going to let us stay out?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Pete, you'll be extended 30 minutes, so you're out for a total of 4 hours.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: And it looks as though you're...
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: All right, how much do we have?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: You've got about 1 hour left, and it looks as though we're going to have to close off pretty much with the nominal plan.
And stand by; we have some words on the traverse back.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: We're standing over at the Head Crater.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Why don't we start picking up some rocks, Pete, while we wait?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Pause) Want to get a picture of that?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let me get it set up.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Pete and Al, two things we'd like you to do on the traverse on the way back: One is to get samples and some documentation of those mounds; and, secondly, if you can, get over to the thousand-foot crater (Middle Crescent Crater), which is northwest of the ALSEP, and get samples and documentation of (those) samples from there.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Is that that one over there?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You don't mean the Head Crater, do you?
(Pause) Let's get some (photos) of this mound, Al.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: If you're at Head Crater now, we'll give you a radar vector (meaning direction and distance).
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: You've already got pictures of this, Pete?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I'm just taking it a close up...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I got it, I got it, I got it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay; let me get a picture of it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let's go around to the other side and not kick any dirt on it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: The crater which we speak of...
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Are you guys going to knock off their relay?
transcript.speaker: Gordon

tokens: Pete, the crater which we speak of (Middle Crescent Crater) is about 300 feet northwest of Head Crater.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: You mean the great big one over here?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Yeah, we can go over there.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: And, Pete and Al, we'll be...
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: ...talking with Yankee Clipper, giving him a maneuver PAD, for about the next 5 minutes.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Let me see if I can chip some of that off, Pete, with this (tool; see the following comment).
By the way, you could work 6 or 7 hours here; (it would) never bother you a bit.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Try to knock a piece of that out.
(Pause) (I) get the feeling that when that crater (Head Crater) was made, it just threw out a big blob of dirt.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Hey, I wouldn't be surprised to find this is that microbreccia.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: We're trying to get a maneuver PAD up to Clipper; and, he's having a hard time copying it with your talking in the background.
Could we have some silence for about 5 minutes, while we get that PAD up?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: We'll be right back with you.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Pete and Al, we're back with you.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: We're almost over to the thousand-foot crater.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Got about another 200 feet to go.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: You can see these linear patterns quite frequently on the surface, Houston.
They seem to generally run from the north to the south, and they're just little lines.
They're off in the dirt; sometimes you see a large area...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: We're in an area right now that looks like it had a fresh impact not too long ago.
Let me take a picture of this one, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Yeah, I got some neat ones right here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Looks like a secondary impact crater that occurred recently.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Some of them do, don't they?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Doesn't have that old look like all the rest do.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: There's some of that glass in the bottom of there.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: We show you're 3 hours and 7 minutes into the EVA.
And we'd like you back to the LM to start the closeout in 10 minutes.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: We're going to have to smoke there, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Yeah, we're almost to the crater.
Okay, we're not getting very many rocks by going this far.
But if that's what you want, that's what you want.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Hey, when we start picking up, we'll try and get a larger sample...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: This looks like a brilliant spanking fresh impact crater!
Look at that little fella, huh?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let's get some rocks right here; here's some.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let me get some pictures first (before they disturb the area they want to sample).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Get some pictures of that crater, and I'll get some over there.
I'll get this one right here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Pause) Wonder why these look so fresh?
Must be just a difference in materials.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Boy, it sure does look fresh though, doesn't it?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Listen, we need to find a grapefruit(-sized rock), too, you know.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Made a dent on this rock.
Wait a minute; I dropped it.
Hold it; move on a little bit; move on; move forward.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Commenting on how quickly they're having to work in order to get back to the LM on time) (I) don't like hustling.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Get right to the edge of this crater and photograph it.
Get a pan in it, and then we won't have to come back this way.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Well, we may want to go back there tomorrow, but we can't go any further.
We'll never get back in 10 minutes.

tokens: Hey, there's bedrock down here a little ways.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I'm going to get in here and...
I got to go to intermediate cooling.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Gonna run just so far on Min.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Why don't you go ahead and pan...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Yeah, let me get to 74 (feet focus).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: You ought to have two fifty (that is, 1/250th of a second exposure).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You ought to have (f/)8 over there, and (f/)11 right there.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Now, let me go back to f/11 (for down-Sun).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We'll have to smoke to get back to that LM.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I was just looking over this rock down here.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Now, let me go over here, and I'll give them a stereo of this baby.

tokens: Let me just leap over here a ways (to his left to get the second pan).

tokens: Houston, we're looking down into this big crater now, and it looks rather old...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Hey, there's some bedrock on the bottom, I think, here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: There's some big boulders that are resting inside the rim.
None on the rim like we see on a large crater that's further to the west by another thousand feet (actually more than 4 kilometers).

But we don't see any outcroppings of rocks either that, you know, we could look down and say, "well, from the top of the rim down to about 20 feet or something, then we come to the underlying rock." But there is this rock that's very large (that is, there are some boulders) and spread around.
We're going to try to collect some of the samples.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Yeah, I've got to get the...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We show you're 3 hours and 11 minutes, and we'd like you back there around 3 (hours) 17 (minutes).

tokens: I can't get it with the tongs.

tokens: Hold my hand and I'll pick it up.

tokens: You got anything else you want to put in your bag?

transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Get a couple of big ones.

tokens: Oh, I wish that we could get that, Al.

tokens: A couple of nice ones right here.

transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: We're getting you some of this rock, Houston.

tokens: We suggest you start smoking on back there.

transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: I was looking at that rock perched right over on top of the hill, there.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Garbled) my distance here, because there's nothing but...

tokens: You could travel a lot further than that, you know it?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You could really make a long traverse if you had a good long-term OPS.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: What do you figure my strides are?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Oh, I'd say (that) each step you're only going about...
When you're running normal, I'd say you go about 3 or 4 feet.
But you could just go indefinitely at this pace.

tokens: Pete, if you land flat-footed and then you just push off your toes and on you go.

tokens: Well, we got that ALSEP up.

tokens: Ease over this way a little.

tokens: I thought there were a couple of good rocks over there.

tokens: Why don't we grab a couple of rocks here?

tokens: Let me get a photograph of it.

tokens: (Pause) (I'll) step in and get the picture.

tokens: (Pause while Pete gets the sample) There you go.

tokens: Let's get one last shot of this thing (the smaller of the two mounds).

tokens: We're (within) about 300 feet of the LM now, Houston.

tokens: Never saw one like that before.

tokens: Put it in the rock bag.

tokens: (Laughs) Here, let's pick up a couple of these.

tokens: (Breathing heavily) Here's our old, friendly Intrepid.

tokens: What do I got to do here?

tokens: I'll tell you something you can do.

tokens: And I'll get the rock box out.

tokens: (Possibly having trouble getting his camera off his
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, let me go get the other two pans, Pete.

tokens: If you have a little trouble, I can help you with it.

transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let me get your rock bag before you get away.

tokens: I'll go get this core tube.

tokens: I wish we had more rocks.

tokens: Pete, you can go ahead and fill up the remainder (of the box) with the fines from that area.

tokens: Houston, we're getting the core tube in real good.
It's down almost full length now.

tokens: It's a little hard to drive in; you have to auger it a bit and then pound it.

tokens: Sounds like you've got the lunar core tube technique worked out.

tokens: I got the record for core tube depth, right now.

tokens: Okay, here comes the core tube.

transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: ...automatic light control switch to Inside, then open the aperture in steps...
transcript.speaker: Gibson

transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: (Pause) Okay, now, I'm going to the aperture.

tokens: Call it out if you would, Al, while you're doing it.

tokens: (Responding to Gibson) Okay, I'm at f/22 right now.
I'll stay right there for about 10 seconds.

I moved it just a little bit, and I'll leave it there for 10 seconds.
Now, I'll tell you when I come to the next marked one.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Houston, rock box 2 (the one for EVA-2)'s going into the Y-pad with the Mylar film - or whatever you call that stuff (a thermal protection blanket) - off the S-band antenna going on top of it.

tokens: Okay, it (the TV) is in (f) 5.6 now, Houston.

tokens: Okay, now it's a little past 5.6.

tokens: I'm turning it (the TV aperture) again, Houston.

tokens: Now it's just a little bit above 2.2.

tokens: Now, it's going to be wide open on the next move, which is right now.

tokens: It's upside down and I'm shaking it now.

tokens: And, Al, why don't you try moving that wire on the back and see if that will do anything?

tokens: I'll try to hold it in for 10 or 15 seconds.

tokens: Let's go with the core tube.

tokens: Okay, now turn around this leg this way...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I can crawl up the ladder halfway if you'll get my upper; and I can crawl up the ladder and knock it off.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: That's a good idea; go ahead and do that.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Just go ahead and start up the ladder.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: You didn't quite make it (to the first rung).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Al, would you give us a mark when you're on the footpad?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: I'm off the footpad right now.
Pete's dusting the boots off, trying to keep some of this dust out of the LM.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Just hold still for a while.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Well, you can get rid of a lot of it, kicking that way...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: ...Get it out of your soles of your boots.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Ohh, I'm in this dingy television wire again.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: All right; I'll help you from back here as best I can.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I'm going to raise my gold visor here so I won't scratch it; and just put my protective visor down.
When you come up, take a good look at the hatch (and look at the scratches it got when they got out at the start of the EVA).
(Pause) I'm really going to have to be tender about it.
I'm not sure we're not going to want to put some tape over it or something.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: That's as far in as I can go.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Maybe your old friendly "Cerd" (that is, CDR) will get the ETB.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let me get my gear (the LEC).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: An ETB and two cameras (inside the ETB) ready to come up.
(Pause) Oh, there's my friend (the TV wire), again.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: That thing's going to drive me buggy.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Do you want me to switch to lunar stay antenna right now while Pete's on the surface?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I'm going to Lunar Stay right now; but we may lose comm for a second.
If I don't hear from you, I'll come back up.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: How do you hear on Lunar Stay?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Sounds like Pete did the job (of erecting the S-Band correctly).
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: (Stops to listen to Gibson) Sure did.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Go ahead and start the ETB up.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (To Gibson) I'm going Track Mode to Off.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Just a second, Pete, I haven't got this ETB (means LEC) rigged.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Matter of fact, you sound stronger, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Wait a second; let me clear a nice little room here for it.
(Pause) Say, are we going to jettison this garbage bag at the end of this EVA, Pete?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Okay, let me get it in good position.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Why don't you throw it out right now?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: And I'll get rid of it (by tossing it under the descent stage).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: That's a good idea; you can move it out of the way.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It won't bother us (during the LEC operations).
Get it when you come up.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Pause) Let me try something that might be just easier on all of us.
I don't have to run one over a pulley; I can just pull it up, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Nah; it's making a mess (by throwing dirt everywhere)!
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Long Pause) Okay, bring her (the LEC hooks) on back, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Don't let it get hung up.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: See, you can't do it that way, Al.
Now, you can let go of it.
(Pause) We got one rock box coming up.
Easy does it; easy does it.
You're pulling when I don't want you to pull!
Now we've got to give it the heave-ho (to get it over the porch lip).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, lift her up a little bit, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: That's right in the door, though!
Let me slide that in here.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: This thing is like playing with a snake.
Get all this good stuff (his end of the LEC) and put it over here in the gear pad.
Now, you're going to hang on to it (Al's end of the LEC) and hand it to me, right?
So I can tie it on the porch?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: What do you want me to do?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: If you've got the ETB and the rock box in, then climb in yourself.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: You all ready for me to come in, Al?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I'm not sure that that Lunar Stay didn't do it.
I'd rather go back on Track and get them back.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Okay, we had a changeover from one site to another, down here.
It was all on our end.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: You guys ought to call those things (the site handovers) out in advance.
That's been happening all the way through this flight.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: And go ahead and press on on the ingress.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: (Jumping up to the first rung) That was easy.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: You might want to do that, too.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let me see if I can find it back here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Gosh, you're shaking the whole LM.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You'll get a warning, because you haven't got your feedwater, but that's okay.
You've got cooling for quite a while.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: All right, hand me the LEC.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, the LEC's got kind of a little slip knot in it because the...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Pete, on your way through the hatch, will you give a check on the seal?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Okay; I can see it probably better than he could from this side, Houston.
I'll tell you what we did do; when we got out, we...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I can't believe I did that.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Because I remember, you were hitting here and then when I backed up.
I'll tell you, we probably just ought to put a piece of tape over it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Hearing a warning tone) That's my feedwater (garbled).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Garbled) the skin on the hatch, and for about a 10-inch cut, there.
And it didn't hurt the insulation; didn't hurt the hatch.
And I don't know whether you want us to put a piece of tape on there or just forget it.
It doesn't look like it's going to bother anything.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Why don't you get over on your side?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I just checked your circuit breakers, and they're all good.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Just a second; I'm not out of the way, yet.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: All right; but I want you to hold the door for a minute while I...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Well, I've got to get out of the way.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let me back up a little better.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Tell me which way to go.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Put your chest down and your rear end up (probably to get the OPS and the top of the PLSS in)...
Go a little bit to the left.
Get a little further (into the cabin) until your head bumps (against the
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You're in the best possible position.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Now careful when you turn around.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Okay; once you turn around, I'll get this hatch.
Okay, it says "PLSS feedwater, close." We did that.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Am I out of the way?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Get down on my hands and knees and lock this thing.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let me raise my visor so I can see what's going on in here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Can you give me a push-me-down?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Just back up and let me tilt further forward, which is just as good.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I can't go back any further.
(Pause) Why don't you let me close it?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, see if you can reach it.
It's just a little bit out of my reach; you might be able to get it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let me get back here in the corner.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: "Dump valve, both Auto." I've verified this one; (and) verified that one.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let me look at something (in the checklist?) first.
(Reading) "PLSS O2 and Press flags may come on during Repress.
If PLSS O2 less than 10 percent,
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Okay; "Lighting Annunciator and Numerics, (change from Dim to) Bright."
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Now I'm going to go over here, (please move) over just a little so I can turn around.
Oh, you did your circuit breakers.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (I've gotten out of the way) the best I can; easy does it.
What are you trying to do?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Well, I can reach the Repress valve, if you want.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: It may be better if you could.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: "Master Alarm and Cabin Repress (warning light comes on)".
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Going ahead, "PLSS O2, Off." (Garbled) That's it; a little bit more.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Can you verify that I've got mine off?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Will you verify that I got mine off?
I'll pull your diverter valve up, and check the water, and you do have it up.
transcript.speaker: Bean
